commands.author: sallyom

target.title: fix: compact Codex OAuth OpenAI sessions without API keys
target.body:
Fixes #95693

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users running canonical OpenAI models through the Codex OAuth runtime could see manual `/compact` or budget-triggered compaction fail with a missing OpenAI API key error, even though normal agent turns used the Codex OAuth runtime successfully.

## Why This Change Was Made

The compaction target resolver now carries an internal `nativeHarnessCompaction` fact when the active OpenAI model should compact through the Codex native harness. Queued compaction uses that fact instead of treating `contextProvider` as the behavior sentinel, so canonical OpenAI + Codex runtime sessions can use native Codex compaction while custom OpenAI-compatible base URL providers continue using the direct OpenClaw compaction path.

## User Impact

Codex/OAuth-only OpenAI sessions can compact without requiring a separate `OPENAI_API_KEY` or API-key-backed profile. This is an internal bug fix only: no new API, SDK, config, CLI flag, or persisted state surface is added, and custom OpenAI-compatible base URL behavior is preserved.

## Evidence

Behavior addressed: Codex OAuth OpenAI compaction no longer falls through to the direct API-key-backed OpenAI compaction path.

Real environment tested: remote Crabbox on Azure Linux, provider `azure`, lease `cbx_fd25a3a71a8b`, run `run_6d25b7e888f2`, label `issue-95693-compaction-proof`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```sh
corepack pnpm vitest run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compaction-runtime-context.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compact.hooks.test.ts --maxWorkers=1
```

Evidence after fix: Crabbox run `run_6d25b7e888f2` passed with `4 passed` test files and `186 passed` tests on the rebased patch. Local post-rebase run of the same command also passed with `4 passed` test files and `186 passed` tests.

Observed result after fix: canonical OpenAI models with `agentRuntime.id = "codex"` select native harness compaction, invoke `maybeCompactAgentHarnessSession`, and avoid requiring an OpenAI API key for that compaction route; custom OpenAI-compatible base URL models continue to avoid native Codex compaction.

What was not tested: no live external Codex OAuth account canary was run. Local Podman Crabbox was attempted first, but the local Podman VM/socket was not running, so the Real Behavior Proof uses remote Azure Crabbox instead.
target.author: sallyom
